title: podman commit gives bad error when container is not running
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
When doing `podman commit` using a container that is NOT running, you receive a container not known error.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  podman pull alpine

2.  podman run alpine /bin/sh

3. podman commit {ctrId}

**Describe the results you received:**
```
# podman commit affectionate_haibt tommer
Error: container not known
```
If I use the same command on a container that's running, it works.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Preferably the commit to happen.  Or if we're not allowing containers that are not running  to be commited, then a better error message and an update in the man page to note that.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
# podman version
Version:            1.7.1-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.7
Git Commit:         eb283657036f5dc69c926432252768d158989100-dirty
Built:              Thu Jan 30 10:58:51 2020
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Sorry about the firedrill baude, I must have had something messed up in my sandbox that created the issue.  I just refreshed it to master, rebuilt and I too can't duplicate.  Closing and apologizing.
